instruction:
An even coin does not stop until three heads appear.
(1) What is the probability of stopping at exactly the sixth time?
(2) What is the probability that the fifth time is also heads, even if the sixth time is stopped?

Options:
A. $$
\frac{17} {32},\ \frac{1} {5}
$$
B. $$
\frac{5} {32},\ \frac{2} {5}
$$
C. $$
\frac{23} {32},\ \frac{1} {5}
$$
D. $$
\frac{7} {32},\ \frac{1} {5}
$$
E. $$
\frac{29} {32},\ \frac{2} {5}
$$
F. $$
\frac{19} {32},\ \frac{2} {5}
$$
G. $$
\frac{1} {32},\ \frac{4} {5}
$$
H. $$
\frac{3} {32},\ \frac{3} {5}
$$
I. $$
\frac{13} {32},\ \frac{1} {5}
$$
J. $$
\frac{5} {32},\ \frac{4} {5}
$$
